category: Using The Vendor Neutral Contingency And Ras Auxiliary File
description: Add Condition to RAS memory model This API routine was first introduced in release 35.4.0. ierr = addcondition(condname, object, condlogic, evaluateinref, disableiftrueinref, timedelay, memo)
python_syntax: ierr = addcondition(condname, object, condlogic, evaluateinref, disableiftrueinref, timedelay, memo)

parameters.name: condlogic
parameters.description: Logic to apply to the list of comparisons (and, or, nand, nor,xor,onetrue). (input).

parameters.name: evaluateinref
parameters.description: Set to yes to cause the model condition to evaluate itself in the reference case and never reevaluate during a contingency solution. (input; NO by default).

parameters.name: disableiftrueinref
parameters.description: Set to yes to disable this model condition for the entire contingency run if it evaluated true in the reference (this is used so that a model condition can be created that checks the status of a branch being opened, but you want to restrict it to triggering an event only when the branch is opened as a result of the contingency). (see model filter explanation in section 5.13.1). (input; NO by default).

parameters.name: timedelay
parameters.description: This field is only defined for the power flow contingency elements. time delay to wait before the defined action takes place specified in a real number in seconds (see note below when using a modelfilter). this is not an absolute time at which an action occurs, but serves as a relative ordering for the implementation of actions. actions with the smallest time delay will be applied first during the topologycheck and postcheck solution steps. (input; 0.0 by default).
